overall_judgment: Parent reward preserves partial_progress but fails to generate high-confidence success_like trajectories; structural deficiencies persist in approach_progress (over-rewarding early_failure) and terminal_reward (misassigning rewards to unsafe landings).
failure_summary: Despite multiple rounds of progress-conditioned mutation, approach_progress still over-rewards early_failure trajectories and terminal_reward assigns positive reward to unsafe landings. No high-confidence success_like trajectories exist; label inconsistency (33 success_like in coverage, 0 in judgment summary) undermines confidence. Training returns have declined in rounds 7-8 after an artifact spike in round 6.

main_known_failures: terminal_reward misassigns positive reward to unsafe landings
main_useful_patterns: partial_progress trajectories abundant with distance improvement
proximity_bonus, near_stability, leg_bonus, low_progress_timeout strongly favor positive (partial_progress) trajectories
uncertainties: No high-confidence success_like trajectories exist; preference construction forbidden
Label inconsistency: coverage reports 33 success_like, trajectory judgment summary shows 0 in 50 samples
Training return spike (760) in round 6 is likely an artifact, not genuine success
Component_summary includes components not in parent code, making diagnosis noisy

component_diagnosis.evidence: Archival lessons and component_summary indicate approach_progress over-rewards early_failure trajectories (failure_mean higher than positive_mean in several rounds). Must be structurally recalibrated to penalize early_failure (e.g., negative bias or quality-based scaling) and widen margin between partial_progress and early_failure.

component_summary shows terminal_reward assigns higher mean to failures (4.375) than positives (2.99), indicating misassignment. Should be replaced with separate crash_penalty (negative for unsafe terminations) and safe_landing_bonus (positive under stringent conditions).
component_summary shows positive_mean (11.32) much higher than failure_mean (3.00), favoring positive trajectories.
component_summary shows positive_mean (14.73) much higher than failure_mean (2.37), favoring positive trajectories.
component_summary shows positive_mean (13.41) much higher than failure_mean (0.80), favoring positive trajectories.
component_summary shows penalty applied only to positives (mean -0.15) but not failures, indicating it correctly penalizes long timeouts with partial progress.
Archival lessons consistently report that progress-conditioned gating preserves partial_progress and enables stage-appropriate rewards.

forbidden_changes: do not add global survival bonus without progress gating
do not construct success_like preference pairs; no high-confidence success_like trajectories
do not treat partial_progress as success_like
required_changes: restructure approach_progress to strongly penalize early_failure trajectories (e.g., negative baseline or quality-based scaling that yields negative reward for low progress or high speed/angle)

replace terminal_reward with separate crash_penalty (sufficiently negative, e.g., -10 or more) for unsafe terminations and safe_landing_bonus (positive under stringent conditions: both legs, very low speed, small angle, sustained leg contact)
preserve progress-conditioned gating (far/near stages) and the positive-favoring components: proximity_bonus, near_stability, leg_bonus, low_progress_timeout
ensure new crash_penalty and safe_landing_bonus are gated by stage (e.g., near_stage) to avoid affecting far-stage behavior

rollback_decision.reason: Hard rollback is handled by RollbackManager; current memory contains weak but usable partial-progress evidence, and decision_level is no_decision, so conservative mutation is recommended. The parent reward is the best current candidate despite structural issues.

self_evaluation_lesson: Progress-conditioned refinements alone are insufficient to overcome structural component deficiencies. Component recomposition is required: restructure approach_progress to penalize early_failure and replace terminal_reward with separate crash_penalty and safe_landing_bonus. Preserve proximity_bonus, near_stability, leg_bonus, low_progress_timeout, and progress-conditioned gating. Maintain conservative mutation strategy due to no_decision level and label inconsistency.
